steps.title: Identify the punctuation constraint
steps.content: The sentence already contains a closing em dash before "was released in 1970." Em dashes used to set off a parenthetical phrase must come in matching pairs: an opening em dash and a closing em dash. Whatever fills the blank must open with an em dash immediately after "Pascal" to create that matching pair.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
Work through each option:
  • Choice A — Pascal—created by Niklaus Wirth: opens with an em dash that pairs with the existing closing em dash, correctly setting off "created by Niklaus Wirth" as a parenthetical. ✓
  • Choice B — Pascal, created by Niklaus Wirth,: uses commas rather than em dashes, mismatching with the closing em dash already in the sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — Pascal created by Niklaus Wirth: omits all punctuation before the phrase, leaving no opener to match the closing em dash. ✗
  • Choice D — Pascal, created by Niklaus Wirth: opens with a comma, creating a comma–em dash mismatch. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
